title: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana
meta_description: PM Suraksha Bima Yojana 2026 — ₹2 lakh accident insurance for just ₹20/year. How to enroll, claim process, coverage details, and comparison with PMJJBY.

hero.one_liner: ₹2 lakh accidental death and disability insurance for just ₹20 per year — India's cheapest accident cover for all bank account holders

what_is_it: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana (PMSBY) is a government-backed accidental death and disability insurance scheme launched on 9 May 2015. For an annual premium of just ₹20 (less than ₹2 per month, or 5 paise per day), it provides ₹2 lakh insurance cover against accidental death, ₹2 lakh for permanent total disability, and ₹1 lakh for permanent partial disability. The scheme is available to all Indian citizens between 18 and 70 years of age who have a savings bank account with Aadhaar linked.

The ₹20 premium is auto-debited from your linked bank account annually between May 25 and July 1. The coverage period is from June 1 to May 31 each year, renewable annually as long as the premium is debited successfully. PMSBY is one of the cheapest insurance products globally — ₹20 per year for ₹2 lakh cover is extraordinary value. Despite the nominal premium, it covers accidental death due to any cause: road accidents, falls, drowning, electrocution, animal attacks, fire, poisoning, or any other accident.

As of January 2026, over 56 crore people have enrolled in PMSBY, making it the world's largest accident insurance scheme by subscriber count. The scheme has paid out claims to millions of families, with average claim processing time being 30-60 days. The ₹2 lakh payout is completely tax-free when paid to the nominee. Every bank in India offers PMSBY enrollment — it's a simple one-page form that takes 5 minutes.

Important distinction: PMSBY covers ONLY accidental death and disability — NOT death from illness or natural causes. If you want comprehensive life cover (covering both illness and accidents), you need PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) at ₹436/year. The ideal combination is both together: PMJJBY (₹436) + PMSBY (₹20) = ₹456/year for ₹4 lakh total cover.

eligibility.label: Age
eligibility.value: 18 to 70 years old. Can enroll at any age within this range; can continue till age 70.

eligibility.label: Bank account
eligibility.value: Must have a savings bank account with Aadhaar linked to it. Post office savings account also eligible.

eligibility.label: Multiple accounts
eligibility.value: If you have accounts in multiple banks, you can enroll through only ONE bank. Duplicate enrollments will be rejected.

eligibility.label: Premium
eligibility.value: ₹20 per year — auto-debited from bank account annually (May 25 – July 1). Keep balance ≥ ₹50.

eligibility.label: Coverage
eligibility.value: Accidental death (₹2L), permanent total disability (₹2L), permanent partial disability (₹1L) only. NOT for illness or natural death.

eligibility.label: Medical exam
eligibility.value: No medical examination, no health questions, no pre-existing condition issues. Anyone aged 18-70 eligible regardless of health.

eligibility.label: Nominee
eligibility.value: You must designate a nominee (family member or trusted person) during enrollment who receives claim if accident occurs.

extra_sections.heading: What PMSBY Covers vs What It Doesn't
rows: Accidental death (road accident, fall, drowning, electrocution, etc.)
Death due to any accident. Most common PMSBY claims.
Loss of both eyes, both hands, both feet, or one eye + one hand/foot. Must be permanent and irreversible.
Loss of one eye OR one hand OR one foot due to accident. Permanent and irreversible.
Death from illness (heart attack, cancer, COVID)
PMSBY is accident-only. Illness deaths are NOT covered.
Death due to old age or unrelated natural event. Not covered.
Disability from disease (stroke, paraplegia from illness)
Only disability from accidents covered. Disability from disease not covered.
Intentional self-injury excluded (standard for all insurance).
If you die while breaking the law. Claim rejected.
Death in war or nuclear event
Standard exclusion. War and nuclear events not covered by any insurance.

extra_sections.heading: PMSBY vs PMJJBY — Know the Difference
rows: Death from ANY cause (illness, accident, natural)
Everyone with dependents needs life cover
Get BOTH for complete family safety

extra_sections.heading: How to Enroll in PMSBY — Easy Step by Step

steps.title: Visit your bank branch or use online method
steps.description: Go to any bank where you have a savings account (SBI, PNB, HDFC, ICICI, BOB, AXIS, or any cooperative bank). Ask for PMSBY enrollment. Alternatively, many banks allow enrollment through: (1) net banking portal, (2) mobile banking app, (3) SMS to bank helpline (SBI: send 'PMSBY Y' to 9223588888), (4) CSC center in your area.

steps.title: Fill one-page consent form
steps.description: Provide your name, bank account number (from passbook), Aadhaar number, and NOMINEE DETAILS: nominee's name, relationship (spouse, child, parent, sibling), and mobile number. Sign the auto-debit authorization form allowing ₹20 annual debit.

steps.title: Premium auto-debited in May-June-July
steps.description: ₹20 is auto-debited from your account between May 25 and July 1 every year. Ensure at least ₹50 balance during this period. If balance is insufficient, debit fails and your coverage lapses. You must re-enroll manually if this happens.

steps.title: Coverage active from June 1
steps.description: Your ₹2 lakh accident insurance is active from June 1 to May 31. You're fully covered from day 1 of enrollment — no waiting period. If you have an accident and die from June 1 onwards, your nominee can claim ₹2 lakh. Coverage auto-renews every June 1 as long as premium is debited.

extra_sections.heading: How to File an Accident Claim — Complete Process

steps.title: Notify bank immediately after accident
steps.description: In case of accidental death or disability: (1) The nominee or the injured person should visit the bank branch where PMSBY is enrolled as soon as possible (within 7 days ideal), (2) Inform bank staff about the accident and request the claim form, (3) Provide basic details of accident: date, location, nature of accident (road accident, fall, drowning, etc.).

steps.title: Collect required documents
steps.description: For DEATH claim: FIR/police report (if accident reported to police), post-mortem report or hospital death certificate, municipal death certificate, nominee's Aadhaar and bank account details, proof of relationship to deceased (if not obvious). For DISABILITY claim: FIR/police report, medical certificate from government hospital confirming permanent disability (e.g., loss of limb), Aadhaar and bank details.

steps.title: Bank forwards claim to insurance company
steps.description: The bank verifies documents and forwards the claim to the insurance partner within 5 days. The insurer reviews: policy active status, accident date within coverage period, eligibility of claim under accident definition, document completeness.

steps.title: Claim approved and amount credited
steps.description: Once verified, ₹2 lakh (death/total disability) or ₹1 lakh (partial disability) is credited to the nominee's or injured person's bank account within 30-60 days. If rejected, written reasons are provided and can be escalated to insurance ombudsman or PMSBY grievance portal.

extra_sections.heading: Why ₹20/Year is the Best Money You'll Spend on Insurance
content: Let's put the value of PMSBY in perspective: ₹20 per year is less than the cost of one cup of tea or a metro ride. For this trivial amount, you get ₹2 lakh insurance cover.

If you earn ₹500/day (₹15,000/month), ₹2 lakh represents 400 days of your income — more than a year's earnings for your family. If you die in a road accident, this ₹2 lakh can: (1) Cover funeral expenses (₹50,000-1 lakh), (2) Pay pending loans (₹50,000-1 lakh), (3) Provide emergency household funds (₹50,000+) for 3-6 months.
For a daily wage laborer, this is transformative. The difference between their family being pushed into debt vs having survival funds is ₹20/year.
Statistically, road accidents, falls, and occupational accidents are the leading cause of death for working-age adults in India — which is exactly what PMSBY covers.

Even if you already have private insurance, PMSBY is worth having because: (1) At ₹20, the cost is negligible, (2) It's additional cover on top of existing insurance, (3) It doesn't require medical underwriting, (4) It covers everyone including high-risk occupations (construction, driving, etc.).
The scheme is so economical that there is literally no reason NOT to have it if you're between 18-70. The only people who shouldn't enroll: those below 18 (not eligible) or above 70 (not eligible).
For everyone else, ₹20 is the cheapest insurance purchase possible.

extra_sections.heading: PMSBY + PMJJBY Bundle — The Complete ₹456 Combo
content: The ultimate protection strategy is enrolling in BOTH schemes simultaneously:
Step 1: Enroll in PMJJBY (₹436/year) — covers death from ANY cause
Step 2: Enroll in PMSBY (₹20/year) — covers accidental death and disability
Total protection: ₹4 lakh (₹2L from PMJJBY + ₹2L from PMSBY if accidental death)

Coverage includes: (1) Death from any cause (heart attack, cancer, illness): ₹2L, (2) Accidental death: ₹4L (if both policies active), (3) Permanent total disability from accident: ₹2L, (4) Permanent partial disability: ₹1L

This ₹456 combination is the foundation of every Indian household's financial safety net. Once you have this base covered, then consider: (1) Children's insurance (Sukanya Samriddhi for girls), (2) Health insurance (Ayushman Bharat for family), (3) Higher-value term insurance (₹50L coverage) if you have dependents, (4) Property/home insurance if you own assets.
Enrollment: Visit your bank once. Ask for both PMJJBY and PMSBY enrollment.
You can complete both in one visit — takes 10 minutes total. Both premiums are auto-debited in May-June from your account.
Two minutes of action, lifetime of protection.

how_to_apply.title: Go to your bank or use net banking
how_to_apply.description: Visit any branch where you have a savings account. Alternatively, enroll through net banking app, mobile app, SMS, or CSC center.

how_to_apply.title: Fill consent form with nominee details
how_to_apply.description: One-page form — your details, nominee name and relationship, consent for ₹20 annual auto-debit.

how_to_apply.title: Ensure ₹50+ balance in May-June-July
how_to_apply.description: Premium is auto-debited between May 25 and July 1 annually. Keep minimum ₹50 balance during this period to avoid debit failure.

how_to_apply.title: Coverage active from June 1
how_to_apply.description: You're covered for accidents from June 1 to May 31. Auto-renews every year via auto-debit. Continue till age 70.

important_alert.text: PMSBY covers ONLY accidental death and disability — NOT illness or natural death. For complete life cover (illness + accident), also enroll in PM Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY) at ₹436/year. Both together = ₹456/year for ₹4 lakh cover (₹2L for any death + ₹2L additional for accidental death).

important_dates.label: Enrollment
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at any bank. Can enroll between ages 18-70.

important_dates.label: Premium debit
important_dates.value: ₹20 auto-debited between May 25 – July 1 annually (keep ₹50+ balance)

important_dates.label: Coverage period
important_dates.value: June 1 to May 31 every year; auto-renews annually

important_dates.label: Coverage age limit
important_dates.value: Can continue till age 70; enrollment closes at 70

important_dates.label: Claim processing
important_dates.value: 30-60 days from complete document submission to payout

faqs.question: Do I need a medical test for PMSBY?
faqs.answer: No. There is absolutely no medical examination, no health questionnaire, and no pre-existing condition exclusion. You can enroll between 18-70 years regardless of health status. Even if you have heart disease, diabetes, or any other condition, you're fully eligible. The only health requirement is being alive and conscious to provide consent.

faqs.question: I have employer insurance. Should I still get PMSBY?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. ₹20/year is so minimal that it's almost free money. PMSBY provides additional cover on top of your employer insurance. If you have ₹5 lakh employer accident coverage + PMSBY, your total accident cover becomes ₹7 lakh. More importantly, employer insurance ends when you change or lose your job — PMSBY continues indefinitely as long as you pay ₹20/year.

faqs.question: What happens if my balance is insufficient for the ₹20 debit?
faqs.answer: Your coverage lapses immediately for that year. You'll have no PMSBY protection until you re-enroll. The insurance company will NOT carry forward the debit — automatic debit is all-or-nothing. If balance is insufficient on May 25, the debit fails completely. You must visit your bank and manually re-enroll to restart coverage.

faqs.question: Can I change the nominee after enrollment?
faqs.answer: Yes. Visit your bank branch and submit a nominee change request with supporting documents (marriage certificate if spouse, birth certificate if child). The updated nominee will be effective from the next coverage year. Keep nominee details current — if your original nominee passes away, update immediately to avoid claim complications.

faqs.question: Is the ₹2 lakh claim amount taxable?
faqs.answer: No. Insurance claim payouts are exempt from income tax under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act. The ₹2 lakh received by the nominee (or ₹1 lakh for partial disability) is completely tax-free. No income tax return filing needed for this amount.

faqs.question: How do I verify if my PMSBY is active?
faqs.answer: Check your bank statement for the ₹20 debit between May-July. If you see the debit, your coverage is active. You can also: (1) Call your bank helpline with account number, (2) Check through net banking/mobile app, (3) Visit bank branch with passbook. Some banks send SMS confirmation of PMSBY renewal.

faqs.question: Can I enroll in PMSBY at multiple banks?
faqs.answer: No. You can enroll through only ONE bank account. If you attempt to enroll through multiple banks, duplicate enrollments will be identified and rejected during claim verification. If you move to a new location, you can close PMSBY at your old bank and open it at a new bank — but not simultaneously at both.

faqs.question: What's the difference between PMSBY and RuPay card accident insurance?
faqs.answer: Both provide ₹2 lakh accident cover, BUT they are separate policies: PMSBY is independent accident insurance with ₹20 annual premium. RuPay card insurance comes free as a feature of your debit card. If you have both active, you can claim from BOTH (₹2L + ₹2L = ₹4L total from both policies combined). The key difference: RuPay insurance requires card usage every 90 days to keep coverage active, while PMSBY just needs annual ₹20 premium payment.
